what_it_does: This file is a proposed interpretive-layer entry for the layout design face: it maps observations from eight source collections onto existing DNA files (layouts, grammar, sequence, tokens, voice), labels each finding as confirmation, new, or conflict, records derived rules, and lists open questions for a human decision-maker.
observations: The layout slice mostly confirms the existing title-main-footer frame across six slide-deck collections, treating product-vi-landing as web-scroll scale evidence rather than a slide-frame instance.
It proposes a new 'takeaway' slot type for the foot zone, described as the slide's thesis or so-what, with plain and emphasized renderings and a non-focal role.
It derives photo-text float placement as a function of the background photo's negative space and tonal contrast, replacing per-instance hand-set coordinates.

It resolves an apparent cross-collection visual-side conflict by keying split-bleed visual placement on narrative role: chaos/problem/before/fragmented-state → left, anchor/resolution/after/rest-point → right.
It flags a symmetric two-panel split as an existing unconsumed proportion ([0.5, 0.5]) that collides with the one-focal-point law, and deliberately does not mint a new archetype.
It routes non-layout findings to their proper faces: pale-green sustainability tint to tokens/color and grammar's foreign-hue quarantine, and inline gold wordmark to voice/atoms.
It confirms existing slot types (stat-band, logo-wall, table, statrow, pills) and the immersive bookends rule, adding broader provenance citations.
It reframes 'one-idea-per-slide' as a content-budget consequence of the one-focal law and sequence measure/fusion tiers, not a layout archetype.
It includes computed_rules that formalize derivations and a list of open_questions_for_tim for unresolved conflicts.
Each augmentation carries target, status, provenance, rationale, and conflict_note fields.
standouts: The 'takeaway' slot — a thesis footer with one role and two emphasis levels — is the headline new layout proposal.
The role-derived visual-side rule for split-bleed resolves a left-vs-right conflict by extending duo's before/after map.
The honest refusal to create a symmetric-split archetype because it violates the one-focal-point law, surfacing the collision instead.
The strict routing of color and voice findings out of the layout face.
conventions: Follows a cross-face routing discipline: layout observations that belong to color, voice, sequence, or grammar are routed and flagged rather than absorbed.
Uses status labels (extends-existing, new, conflicts-with-existing) and conflict_note fields to separate confirmation from addition from collision.
Cites provenance with collection and finding numbers (e.g., recent-pitches #32, company-info #8).
Respects the derive-never-place principle by turning per-instance placements into functions of image or role.
Breaks from simply listing new archetypes by explicitly NOT minting one when it would collide with a stated design law.
concerns: Several key rules are marked as Inferred or derived-from-principle rather than directly observed, which may need validation.
The file repeatedly references an external 'FOCUS' brief and '36-finding slice' that are not included here, so context is incomplete.
Some computed_rules are framed as candidates for a decision-maker ('for Tim'), leaving the file partly as a question list rather than a finished proposal.
The 'takeaway' emphasized rendering mentions both hatch and tinted-cream surfaces without resolving which surface token to use.
The symmetric-split discussion introduces a 'register dial' concept that is not defined in this file.
notes: A layout-face interpretation that is mostly in the business of confirming existing rules, adding one new slot, deriving two placement rules, and honestly routing or rejecting findings that belong elsewhere.
questions: What is the 'FOCUS' brief and where does it live?
What are the exact schemas of the targeted dna/*.json files (layouts, grammar, sequence, tokens, voice)?
What process or authority turns the open_questions_for_tim into approved rules?
What does 'register dial' refer to, and how is it encoded?
How are the source collection finding numbers (#1-#38) produced and validated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it presents itself as a neutral, file-local interpretive layer for a design face and does exactly that: interprets, routes, confirms, conflicts, and asks questions without ranking.
intention_signals: The file is trying to translate raw collection findings into a disciplined, law-aware design-system update: it wants to add only what genuinely belongs to layout, derive rules so they generalize, and pass every hard decision back to the system's author.
novelty: The combination of a non-focal 'takeaway' thesis slot with two emphasis levels, a role-based visual-side derivation for split-bleed, and the explicit treatment of an unconsumed symmetric proportion as a law collision rather than a missing archetype.
contribution: It would contribute the proposed 'takeaway' slot and its placement in the foot zone, the photo-text offset derivation, the role-based split-bleed side rule, a broadened provenance record for confirmed slot types and frames, the routing discipline for non-layout findings, and a set of flagged conflicts and open questions for a fused system to resolve.
summary_for_embedding: A proposed interpretive-layer entry for the layout design face that confirms the title-main-footer frame and existing slot types, proposes a non-focal 'takeaway' thesis slot, derives photo-text float and split-bleed visual-side placement from image/role, routes color and voice findings to their own faces, and flags a symmetric-split proportion as conflicting with the one-focal law.
intention_for_embedding: To translate source-collection layout observations into a law-aware, cross-face-routed set of confirmations, additions, derivations, and unresolved questions for the design-system ledger.
